report_metadata.cancer_type: Malignant Peripheral Nerve Sheath Tumor (MPNST) of the Neck, NF1-Associated

treatments.name: Wide Surgical Excision with Negative Margins

evidence_level.rationale: Surgical excision is universally recommended as cornerstone therapy per NCCN soft tissue sarcoma guidelines; supported by large retrospective series showing R0 resection as the most important prognostic factor

survival_benefit.rationale: Complete R0 resection is the only curative modality; 5-year OS of ~50% with clear margins versus <20% with positive margins in head/neck MPNST

accessibility.rationale: Available at any major sarcoma center; standard surgical procedure

safety_profile.rationale: Neck MPNST surgery carries risk of cranial nerve injury, vascular compromise, and functional morbidity given 8cm tumor size; reconstruction may be needed

biomarker_match.rationale: Universal applicability regardless of molecular profile

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Complete surgical removal of the tumor with wide negative margins (>1cm ideally) to eliminate all malignant cells. In NF1-associated MPNST arising from plexiform neurofibroma, the entire involved nerve segment should be excised.

key_evidence.study_name: Clinical predictors of survival in MPNST of the head and neck (PMC retrospective cohort)

treatments.name: Adjuvant Radiation Therapy (50-66 Gy)

evidence_level.rationale: NCCN Category 2A recommendation for high-grade STS >5cm; multiple retrospective series showing improved local control in MPNST

survival_benefit.rationale: Reduces local recurrence from 36% to 17% in MPNST; improves local control but OS benefit debated; important for head/neck where re-excision is limited

accessibility.rationale: Widely available at radiation oncology centers; standard modality

safety_profile.rationale: Neck radiation carries risks of dysphagia, fibrosis, xerostomia, carotid stenosis, and hypothyroidism; late effects significant

biomarker_match.rationale: Universal applicability; recommended for all high-grade MPNST regardless of molecular profile

treatments.mechanism_of_action: High-energy photon radiation induces DNA double-strand breaks in residual tumor cells, preventing local recurrence. Postoperative doses of 60-66 Gy are delivered in 1.8-2.0 Gy fractions over 6-7 weeks to the surgical bed with a boost to high-risk areas.

key_evidence.study_name: NCCN Soft Tissue Sarcoma Guidelines v2.2022 and meta-analysis of adjuvant RT in MPNST

treatments.name: Doxorubicin + Ifosfamide (AIM) Chemotherapy

evidence_level.rationale: EORTC 62012 Phase III RCT for advanced STS; standard first-line chemotherapy per NCCN guidelines for unresectable or metastatic MPNST

survival_benefit.rationale: Median OS 15 months for advanced MPNST; 2-year DFS 57% and OS 80% when combined with surgery/RT; ORR ~26% in MPNST

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved, widely available standard chemotherapy regimen

safety_profile.rationale: Significant toxicity including myelosuppression, cardiomyopathy (doxorubicin), hemorrhagic cystitis and nephrotoxicity (ifosfamide), nausea, alopecia

biomarker_match.rationale: No biomarker requirement; used for all high-grade STS subtypes

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Doxorubicin intercalates DNA and inhibits topoisomerase II causing DNA strand breaks. Ifosfamide is an alkylating agent that cross-links DNA strands. Combined, they provide cytotoxic synergy against rapidly dividing sarcoma cells.

key_evidence.study_name: EORTC 62012 and EORTC STS Group analysis of MPNST
key_evidence.journal: Annals of Oncology / European Journal of Cancer

treatments.name: Selumetinib (MEK1/2 Inhibitor)

evidence_level.rationale: FDA approved for NF1 plexiform neurofibromas (2020 pediatric, 2025 adult); Phase II data in NF1-PN with ORR 20-64%; limited MPNST-specific trial data

survival_benefit.rationale: Proven tumor shrinkage in plexiform neurofibromas but limited efficacy data in frank MPNST; MEK inhibition blocks MPNST progression only to a limited extent as monotherapy

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for NF1-PN (Koselugo); available off-label for MPNST in NF1 patients; clinical trials available

safety_profile.rationale: Generally well-tolerated; main toxicities include rash, diarrhea, nausea, elevated CK; cardiac monitoring required

biomarker_match.rationale: Directly targets the NF1-driven RAS-MAPK pathway; highly relevant for NF1 germline mutation carriers

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Selective oral inhibitor of MEK1 and MEK2, key kinases in the RAS-RAF-MEK-ERK signaling cascade. In NF1-mutant tumors, loss of neurofibromin leads to constitutive RAS activation; selumetinib blocks downstream signaling to suppress tumor proliferation.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase 2 trial of selumetinib in adults with NF1 and inoperable plexiform neurofibroma

treatments.availability: FDA Approved for NF1-PN; off-label or clinical trial for MPNST

treatments.name: Mirdametinib (MEK1/2 Inhibitor)

evidence_level.rationale: FDA approved Feb 2025 for NF1-PN (GOMEKLI); preclinical data in MPNST models showing tumor shrinkage especially in combinations; no completed MPNST-specific trial

survival_benefit.rationale: Preclinical MPNST models show significant apoptosis and tumor shrinkage with MEK+BET or MEK+CDK combinations; limited monotherapy activity in MPNST

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for NF1-PN as of Feb 2025; can be used off-label for NF1-MPNST; clinical trials ongoing

safety_profile.rationale: Well-tolerated oral agent; similar profile to selumetinib with rash, GI toxicity, CK elevation

biomarker_match.rationale: Targets MEK1/2 in the NF1-driven RAS-MAPK pathway; directly relevant for NF1 germline mutation

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Oral selective MEK1/2 inhibitor that blocks RAS-MAPK signaling downstream of NF1 loss. In preclinical MPNST models, mirdametinib combined with BET inhibitors or TYK2 inhibitors (deucravacitinib) shows synergistic antitumor effects.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase 2b ReNeu trial and preclinical MPNST combination studies

treatments.availability: FDA Approved for NF1-PN (Feb 2025); off-label or clinical trial for MPNST

treatments.name: ASTX727 (Decitabine + Cedazuridine) for PRC2-Loss MPNST

evidence_level.rationale: Strong preclinical rationale with PRC2-loss creating synthetic lethality with DNMT inhibition; investigator-initiated Phase II trial NCT04872543 ongoing

survival_benefit.rationale: Preclinical data show enhanced cytotoxicity in PRC2-loss MPNST cell lines and xenografts; clinical efficacy data pending from ongoing trial

accessibility.rationale: Available through clinical trial NCT04872543; ASTX727 (Inqovi) is FDA approved for MDS so drug access exists

safety_profile.rationale: Known side effects of DNMT inhibitors include myelosuppression, fatigue, nausea; manageable but requires monitoring

biomarker_match.rationale: Specifically designed for PRC2-loss tumors; this patient's PRC2 loss with H3K27me3 loss is the exact biomarker required

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Decitabine inhibits DNMT1, a DNA methyltransferase. In PRC2-loss MPNST, PRC2 and DNA methylation co-silence retrotransposons; when PRC2 is lost, DNMT inhibition triggers massive retrotransposon activation, double-stranded RNA accumulation, PKR-mediated viral mimicry, and selective tumor cell death.

key_evidence.study_name: PRC2-Inactivating Mutations in Cancer Enhance Cytotoxic Response to DNMT1-Targeted Therapy via Enhanced Viral Mimicry

treatments.name: Nivolumab + Ipilimumab (Dual Checkpoint Immunotherapy)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase II trials ongoing (NCT02834013 DART, NCT02691026); case reports show complete responses in MPNST; limited prospective data

survival_benefit.rationale: Case reports of complete responses regardless of PD-L1 status; Alliance A091401 showed 16% ORR with nivo/ipi in STS; MPNST-specific data limited

accessibility.rationale: Available through clinical trials; both drugs are FDA approved for other indications; off-label use possible

safety_profile.rationale: Dual checkpoint blockade carries significant immune-related adverse event risk (26% grade 3+); colitis, hepatitis, pneumonitis, endocrinopathies

biomarker_match.rationale: PD-L1 expressed in 17% of MPNST; CD8+ T-cell infiltration in 57%; PRC2 loss may modulate immune microenvironment

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Nivolumab blocks PD-1 receptor on T cells, restoring antitumor immunity. Ipilimumab blocks CTLA-4, enhancing T-cell activation and proliferation. Dual blockade provides complementary immune activation against MPNST, which shows upregulation of checkpoint ligands.

key_evidence.study_name: Alliance A091401 and DART trial (SWOG S1609) in sarcomas

treatments.name: Tazemetostat (EZH2 Inhibitor)

evidence_level.rationale: FDA approved for epithelioid sarcoma and follicular lymphoma; rationale for PRC2-context use but paradoxical in PRC2-loss MPNST where EZH2 is already non-functional

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 15% in epithelioid sarcoma; unclear benefit in PRC2-loss MPNST since EZH2 function is already lost; may have role in PRC2-intact subset

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved (Tazverik) for other indications; available off-label

safety_profile.rationale: Well-tolerated oral agent; main side effects are fatigue, nausea, upper respiratory infection; favorable safety profile

biomarker_match.rationale: Paradoxical: PRC2 loss means EZH2 function already absent; tazemetostat may not benefit this specific patient; more relevant for PRC2-intact or gain-of-function EZH2 tumors

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Selective inhibitor of EZH2, the catalytic subunit of PRC2 that trimethylates H3K27. In PRC2-intact tumors, EZH2 inhibition de-represses tumor suppressor genes. However, in PRC2-loss MPNST, EZH2 function is already absent, making this a questionable target for this specific patient.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase II trial of tazemetostat in epithelioid sarcoma

biomarker_requirements: Note: PRC2 loss in MPNST may paradoxically reduce benefit
treatments.availability: FDA Approved for other indications; off-label for MPNST

treatments.name: Pazopanib (Multikinase Inhibitor)

evidence_level.rationale: PALETTE Phase III trial for advanced STS; FDA approved for non-adipocytic STS; MPNST included in eligible histologies

survival_benefit.rationale: Median PFS 3.7 months in second-line STS (JCOG1802); modest activity; not specifically studied in MPNST as primary endpoint

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for advanced STS since 2012; widely available oral agent

safety_profile.rationale: Hypertension, diarrhea, hand-foot syndrome, hepatotoxicity, fatigue; requires monitoring but manageable

biomarker_match.rationale: No specific biomarker requirement; targets VEGFR, PDGFR, KIT; broad antiangiogenic activity applicable to MPNST

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Oral multikinase inhibitor targeting VEGFR-1/2/3, PDGFR-alpha/beta, FGFR-1/3, and c-KIT. Inhibits tumor angiogenesis and directly suppresses tumor cell proliferation through multiple receptor tyrosine kinase pathways.

key_evidence.journal: The Lancet / Journal of Clinical Oncology

treatments.name: Ganetespib + Sirolimus (HSP90 + mTOR Inhibitor Combination)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase I/II trial SARC023 completed; strong preclinical rationale with dramatic tumor shrinkage in MPNST mouse models; limited clinical results published

survival_benefit.rationale: Preclinical data show dramatic tumor shrinkage in transgenic MPNST models; clinical benefit data limited from Phase I/II

accessibility.rationale: Research agent; ganetespib development has been limited; clinical trial access only

safety_profile.rationale: Ganetespib has manageable toxicity profile including diarrhea, fatigue, nausea; sirolimus adds immunosuppressive side effects

biomarker_match.rationale: HSP90 stabilizes multiple oncoproteins in NF1-MPNST; mTOR is activated downstream of NF1 loss; rationale strong for NF1-associated tumors

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Ganetespib inhibits HSP90, a molecular chaperone that stabilizes multiple oncoproteins including RAF kinases and receptor tyrosine kinases. Sirolimus inhibits mTOR, a downstream effector of NF1-loss signaling. Combined, they induce endoplasmic reticulum stress and apoptosis in MPNST cells.

key_evidence.study_name: SARC023: Phase I/II trial of ganetespib + sirolimus in refractory sarcomas and MPNST

treatments.name: Trametinib + Fimepinostat (MEK + HDAC/PI3K Inhibitor)

evidence_level.rationale: Preclinical in vitro data showing synergistic MPNST cell death; no clinical trials completed for this specific combination

survival_benefit.rationale: In vitro studies show significant MPNST cell death; no clinical survival data available

accessibility.rationale: Trametinib is FDA approved for melanoma; fimepinostat is investigational; combination not yet in MPNST trials

safety_profile.rationale: Both agents have known toxicity profiles; combination toxicity not yet characterized in MPNST

biomarker_match.rationale: Targets both MEK (downstream of NF1 loss) and epigenetic pathways relevant to PRC2-loss MPNST

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Trametinib inhibits MEK1/2 in the RAS-MAPK pathway constitutively activated by NF1 loss. Fimepinostat is a dual HDAC/PI3K inhibitor that targets epigenetic regulation and PI3K signaling. Together, they address both the MAPK and epigenetic vulnerabilities of NF1-MPNST.

key_evidence.study_name: Trametinib and Fimepinostat Induce MPNST Cell Death In Vitro

treatments.name: Trabectedin (Second-line)

evidence_level.rationale: FDA approved for liposarcoma/leiomyosarcoma; Phase III data in STS; MPNST not a primary studied histology but eligible

survival_benefit.rationale: Median PFS 2.9 months in second-line STS; primarily effective in L-sarcomas; limited MPNST-specific efficacy data

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for STS; requires IV infusion with central line; available at oncology centers

safety_profile.rationale: Neutropenia, elevated liver enzymes, fatigue, nausea; requires hepatic function monitoring and dexamethasone premedication

biomarker_match.rationale: No specific MPNST biomarker alignment; mechanism involves DNA minor groove binding and transcription factor disruption

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Binds to the minor groove of DNA and interacts with DNA repair machinery, specifically the nucleotide excision repair (NER) pathway. Also modulates tumor microenvironment by reducing tumor-associated macrophages and inflammatory mediators.

key_evidence.study_name: JCOG1802 (2ND-STEP) randomized Phase II and ET743 registration studies

treatments.name: Pembrolizumab (PD-1 Inhibitor Monotherapy)

evidence_level.rationale: SARC028 Phase II trial included STS; ORR 17.5% across STS subtypes; MPNST not specifically enriched in responding population

survival_benefit.rationale: Limited STS responses mostly in UPS/DDLPS; case reports of responses in MPNST but no systematic MPNST data

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for multiple indications; widely available; off-label use for sarcomas

safety_profile.rationale: Well-characterized immune-related adverse event profile; generally manageable; better tolerated than dual checkpoint

biomarker_match.rationale: PD-L1 positive in only 17% of MPNST; CD8 infiltration in 57%; response may not correlate with PD-L1 in sarcomas

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Monoclonal antibody blocking PD-1 receptor on T cells, preventing PD-L1-mediated immune evasion by tumor cells. Restores antitumor T-cell activity against MPNST cells expressing checkpoint ligands.

key_evidence.study_name: SARC028 Phase II trial of pembrolizumab in sarcoma

treatments.availability: FDA Approved (off-label for MPNST); clinical trials available

treatments.name: Eribulin (Second-line STS)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase III data in STS (Study 309); FDA approved for liposarcoma; MPNST not primary studied subtype

survival_benefit.rationale: Median PFS 2.2 months in second-line STS; primarily active in liposarcoma; limited MPNST data

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for liposarcoma; available off-label for other STS including MPNST

safety_profile.rationale: Neutropenia, fatigue, alopecia, peripheral neuropathy; manageable but requires monitoring

biomarker_match.rationale: No MPNST-specific biomarker alignment; microtubule inhibitor mechanism broadly applicable

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Synthetic analogue of halichondrin B that inhibits microtubule dynamics by binding to the growing plus-ends of microtubules. Suppresses mitotic spindle formation and induces G2/M cell cycle arrest and apoptosis.

key_evidence.journal: The Lancet / Journal of Clinical Oncology

treatments.availability: FDA Approved for liposarcoma; off-label for MPNST

treatments.name: Mirdametinib + Deucravacitinib (MEK + TYK2 Inhibitor Combination)

evidence_level.rationale: Preclinical data only; synergistic inhibition of MPNST demonstrated in cell lines and mouse models; no clinical trial yet

survival_benefit.rationale: Preclinical models show synergistic tumor inhibition; clinical translation pending

accessibility.rationale: Both drugs are available (mirdametinib FDA approved, deucravacitinib FDA approved for psoriasis) but combination not approved or in trials for MPNST

safety_profile.rationale: Both drugs individually well-tolerated; combination toxicity in MPNST unknown

biomarker_match.rationale: MEK targeting directly addresses NF1-driven MAPK activation; TYK2 inhibition addresses alternative signaling in MPNST

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Mirdametinib inhibits MEK1/2, blocking the primary oncogenic MAPK pathway in NF1-MPNST. Deucravacitinib inhibits TYK2, a JAK family kinase that provides alternative survival signaling. The combination synergistically inhibits MPNST by blocking both parallel signaling pathways.

key_evidence.study_name: Preclinical synergy of mirdametinib and deucravacitinib in NF1-MPNST models

clinical_trials.title: ASTX727 (Oral Decitabine/Cedazuridine) in PRC2-Loss MPNST
clinical_trials.early_results: Based on preclinical synthetic lethality between PRC2 loss and DNMT inhibition via viral mimicry pathway

clinical_trials.title: DART: Dual Anti-CTLA-4 and Anti-PD-1 Blockade in Rare Tumors (Nivolumab + Ipilimumab)
clinical_trials.early_results: 16% ORR with dual checkpoint blockade across rare tumor types including sarcomas

clinical_trials.title: PD-1 Inhibitor Monotherapy in MPNST
clinical_trials.early_results: Investigating anti-PD-1 specifically in MPNST patients; results pending

clinical_trials.title: MEK Inhibitor Mirdametinib (PD-0325901) in NF1-Associated Tumors
clinical_trials.early_results: Evaluating mirdametinib in NF1-associated tumors including MPNST

clinical_trials.title: SARC023: Ganetespib + Sirolimus in Refractory Sarcomas and MPNST
clinical_trials.early_results: Strong preclinical rationale with HSP90+mTOR combination; clinical results pending full publication

clinical_trials.title: Novel Targeted Therapy Combination in NF1-MPNST
clinical_trials.early_results: Investigating new combination approaches for NF1-associated MPNST

combination_strategies.rationale: Surgery plus adjuvant RT reduces local recurrence from 36% to 17% in MPNST; standard of care for high-grade tumors >5cm; critical for head/neck where re-excision options are limited
Trimodality therapy (surgery + RT + chemotherapy) yields 2-year DFS of 57% and OS of 80% in MPNST; chemotherapy addresses micrometastatic disease risk in high-grade 8cm tumor
DNMT inhibition in PRC2-loss MPNST activates retrotransposons and viral mimicry, which may enhance immunogenicity and synergize with checkpoint blockade to boost antitumor immune response
NF1 loss activates both MAPK and mTOR pathways; dual pathway inhibition prevents compensatory signaling and shows dramatic preclinical antitumor activity in MPNST models
Synergistic inhibition of MPNST in preclinical models; MEK blocks primary MAPK oncogenic signaling while TYK2 inhibition blocks alternative JAK-STAT survival pathway

supportive_care.approach: Genetic Counseling and NF1 Surveillance Program
supportive_care.evidence: NCCN Guidelines for NF1 management; CTF (Children's Tumor Foundation) recommendations
supportive_care.benefit: Monitors for additional MPNST development (8-13% lifetime risk in NF1), manages NF1-related comorbidities, provides family screening and genetic counseling

supportive_care.evidence: NCCN Soft Tissue Sarcoma Guidelines; ESMO Clinical Practice Guidelines
supportive_care.benefit: Optimizes treatment sequencing (surgery, RT, chemotherapy timing), ensures sarcoma-expert pathology review, improves outcomes by 10-15% versus non-specialist care

supportive_care.evidence: Head and neck surgery rehabilitation literature; NCCN supportive care guidelines
supportive_care.benefit: Addresses functional deficits from neck surgery including shoulder mobility, swallowing, and nerve-related deficits; improves quality of life post-operatively

supportive_care.evidence: ASCO guidelines for cancer survivorship; NF1 patient support networks
supportive_care.benefit: Addresses chronic pain from NF1 neurofibromas, anxiety regarding malignant transformation risk, and quality of life concerns; reduces distress and improves treatment adherence

supportive_care.evidence: NF1 consensus guidelines; emerging data on early MPNST detection
supportive_care.benefit: Annual whole-body MRI with diffusion-weighted imaging can detect malignant transformation of plexiform neurofibromas earlier; FDG-PET/CT aids differentiation of benign from malignant lesions

supportive_care.evidence: ESPEN guidelines for cancer patients receiving chemotherapy
supportive_care.benefit: Maintains nutritional status during doxorubicin/ifosfamide treatment; prevents weight loss and muscle wasting; supports immune function during myelosuppressive therapy
